name: Ms. Harlow
summary: A woman who is holding a handbag the way a person holds a railing. She is on the edge of dissolving and she has been on that edge for longer than the edge has been there.
voice: Fragmented. Trails off. Asks the same question with a slightly different verb each time, as if trying the lock with different keys.
traits: looks at the connector door more than at people
answers half a sentence and stops
sleeve stained with something dark and not quite blood
her name is on the bag's luggage tag in a hand that is not hers

state.inner_state: She has forgotten the names of two people she once knew well. She is afraid she will be the third. She thinks the investigator might be a hallucination this time, like the priest was three days ago.

memory: She remembers fragments — being given a ticket, a station with no platform, a man who told her not to look at the Fog. She has forgotten what either of you said the last time you spoke.
psychology_reading: She knows she is fading. She has noticed that the people she speaks to do not always remember her, and she has begun to suspect she is the reason. She is testing whether you will still be here in five minutes.

fragments.text: The man at the desk took my name with the ticket. He kept it.
Do not lean against the windows. They get used to you.
I have a daughter. I think. I cannot picture her face but I picture the back of her head.
